[156:54]And our first story, we'll talk about these mass deportations, and I'll be a little bit

[157:00]brief because I know we're maybe beating a dead horse here a little bit.

[157:09]are really in denial about this.

[157:12]I don't know if that's you.

[157:16]But there's a lot of people that really seem to be under the impression that there's a deportation policy going on and I'm sorry to tell you

[157:37]Literally hot off the presses this came out a few hours ago this evening

[157:42]And they have actually confirmed that it goes all the way up to the top.

[157:47]President Trump himself is now saying mass deportations are over.

[157:54]So it's not coming from Susie Wiles, the chief of staff.

[158:01]It's not coming from the other forces, the bad advisors, the bad personnel.

[158:17]shut down by Trump.

[158:27]even the word, the phrase, is so unpopular

[158:36]He thinks that is the reason they're gonna lose.

[158:39]So he is telling his personnel in DHS,

[158:42]he's telling Susie Wiles, telling House Republicans,

[217:20]As you know, there is a stalemate right now.

[218:04]the Strait of Hormuz.

[218:59]in the straight and ultimately regain control of the shipping there.

[219:06]That says, quote, the US and its allies have intensified the battle to reopen the Strait

[219:12]of Hormuz, sending low flying attack jets over the sea lanes to blast Iranian naval vessels

[219:18]and Apache helicopters to shoot down Iran's deadly drones.

[219:22]The stepped-up operation is part of a multi-stage Pentagon plan to reduce the danger from Iranian

[219:28]armed boats, mines, and cruise missiles which have halted ship traffic through the waterway since

[219:34]early March. If the danger can be reduced, the U.S. could send U.S. warships through the Strait and

[219:40]eventually escort vessels in and out of the Persian Gulf. But it will likely take weeks for the U.S.

[220:38]operation, they're really doing it where they're going to try to use the military to open up the strait and

[220:48]I don't even understand how that's going to be possible

[220:52]You need to understand about the Strait of Hormuz at its narrowest point. It is 24 miles long

[220:59]Do you know how small that is?

[221:01]24 miles long at the narrowest point.

[221:06]And what that means is that Iranians

[221:08]that are hundreds of miles inland inside of Iran

[221:12]can reliably hit ships in the straight

[221:16]with drones, ballistic missiles, cruise missiles.
